steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The researchers conclude that deceptive display prevalence is mediated by absolute latitude of brooding sites, independent of avian order. Supporting evidence must show the percentage of species using displays rising or falling consistently with latitude — across orders, not just within one.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content: The response about where 54% of the birds brood only describes location, not the percentage using displays at each latitude band. The two responses centered on Charadriiformes make the pattern depend on order, which conflicts with the conclusion that latitude matters independently of order. The correct response compares display prevalence across all orders: about 34% for brooding ranges of $0^{\circ}$-$30^{\circ}$ absolute latitude versus about 60% for $50^{\circ}$-$80^{\circ}$. That increase supports the researchers' claim. D is correct.
